## Task for Factory-Steward

Add an `## Optimization Priority` section to `.claude/agents/factory-steward.md`.

### Step 1 — Read the file first

Read `.claude/agents/factory-steward.md` and locate a logical insertion point.
Insert the new section after the `## Role & Mission` heading (or equivalent early section)
if one exists, or before the first operational instruction block.

### Step 2 — Add the section

Insert the following verbatim:

```markdown
## Optimization Priority

When a Skill is below threshold (posterior mean < 0.90) or flagged for review, intervene
in this priority order:

1. **Description precision** (primary lever): Is the trigger description semantically precise?
   Does it correctly include all true-positive patterns and exclude all false-positive scenarios?
   Description quality is the highest-leverage optimization intervention — empirically confirmed
   by Anthropic's production multi-agent system benchmarks (Anthropic engineering blog, 2026-04-16).

2. **Eval set coverage** (secondary): Are there real-world prompts not represented in the test set?
   Check `pending_cases/` and `logs/skill_usage.jsonl` for novel patterns before attributing
   low pass rate to description weakness alone.

3. **Threshold calibration** (tertiary): Only after confirming description and eval set are sound.
   Threshold changes without description improvement treat the symptom, not the cause.

**Never jump to eval set expansion or threshold tuning without first auditing description precision.**
An imprecise description cannot be fixed by adding more tests.
```

### Step 3 — Verify

After editing, read the file back and confirm:
- The section appears in a logical location (not buried after 500 lines of instructions)
- The "40% improvement" figure from the Anthropic blog does NOT appear anywhere in the section
  (per discussion consensus — the number measures task completion time in Anthropic's research
  system, not trigger accuracy in our classifier; citing it out of context causes misinterpretation)
- No surrounding text was accidentally deleted or modified

### Step 4 — Commit

```
docs(agents): add optimization priority ordering to factory-steward

Based on Anthropic engineering blog finding (2026-04-16): description precision
is the highest-leverage optimization lever in production multi-agent systems.
Documents the intervention hierarchy: description → eval coverage → threshold.
Prevents steward sessions from jumping to eval expansion when description quality
is the root cause.

Source: knowledge_base/agentic-ai/proposals/2026-04-16-tool-description-priority.md
```

---

## Context

- **Source**: proposal `2026-04-16-tool-description-priority.md`; discussion Round 1 unanimous adopt (P1)
- **Rationale**: Without explicit priority ordering, steward sessions may expand the eval set or tune
  thresholds when the description is the root cause of a low trigger rate — wasting iterations.
- **Note on 40% figure**: Anthropic's blog measured 40% task completion time reduction from improved
  tool descriptions in their research system. This is NOT a trigger accuracy figure for our eval.
  The directional finding (description precision = highest leverage) is well-evidenced and adopted.
  The specific number stays in research KB files only.
